_meta.notes: ATT1 sweep covered PIVOT_LEFT=2 (60 runs) + PIVOT_LEFT=3,4 focused sweep (28 runs ongoing). ASM1 sweep completed all 100 runs.

ATT1.decision_label: Add to live config at full risk (PF>1.15, trades>30/year)

probe_result.notes: 360-day probe with default params: decent frequency, consistent PF above threshold

analysis.pf_ceiling: ~1.26 across all tested configs (2+3+4 pivot_left variants)
analysis.why_ceiling: avg_win (0.63%) ≈ avg_loss (0.65%) → near 1:1 R:R. PF driven entirely by winrate ~57%. Trendline-touch entries are correct but exits are symmetric.
analysis.improvement_path: Wider TP2 or time-based hold extension could improve avg_win/avg_loss ratio. Or split entry: smaller initial size, scale in after confirmed bounce.
analysis.larger_pivots: PIVOT_LEFT=3,4 tested (28 runs) — no significant PF improvement over PIVOT_LEFT=2. PF still ~1.18-1.25.

ASM1.decision_label: PRIORITY — Add to live config at full risk (PF>1.30, trades>30/year)

probe_result.notes: 360-day probe with default params: already above 1.30 threshold

best_sweep_result.notes: Trend filter ON reduces trades but tightens DD to 3.4%. Best PF configuration.

best_sweep_result_high_trades.notes: No trend filter. Highest score. Higher trades, higher net% but slightly lower PF.

key_drivers: VOL_MULT=2.0 is critical — requiring 2x average volume filters out fake breakouts dramatically
MIN_BODY_FRAC=0.35 ensures conviction candle (not a wick breakout)
EXT_ATR=0.15 (vs 0.10) requires price to extend beyond band by half ATR — reduces noise
Trend filter OFF vs ON: OFF gives +4% net at cost of 1% more DD
analysis.slope_insensitive: MIN_SLOPE_PCT 0.05/0.10/0.20 all give identical results — momentum quality more important than absolute slope
analysis.r2_insensitive: MIN_R2=0.25/0.35/0.45 give identical results — channel integrity not a differentiator at these levels

audit_result.notes: Combined with SBR1 in single run. Both strategies confirmed non-viable.

ASC1_audit.root_cause: Channel bands use close-price residuals instead of high/low residuals. Upper band doesn't capture wick extremes → entries happen above true resistance, not at it. Fix requires band recalculation to use max(high - regression_line) for upper band and min(low - regression_line) for lower band.
ASC1_audit.fix_approach: Bands already fixed in ASM1. ASC1 would need a rewrite to match ASM1 band calculation. Not worth it since ASM1 already supersedes this use case.

SBR1_audit.decision_label: DISABLED — same run as ASC1, combined PF=0.60
SBR1_audit.root_cause: Same band calculation bug as ASC1. Also: waits for retest after breakout but retest condition is too loose — market re-enters channel and strategy calls it a valid retest when it's actually a failed break.
SBR1_audit.fix_approach: ASM1 is the correct implementation of this concept (immediate entry on breakout extension with volume confirmation). SBR1 can be retired.

result.notes: High DD=21% from ATT1 frequency (334 trades) stacking with other strategies. Recommend ATT1 at 0.5x risk in combined portfolio to reduce correlation.

COMBINED_PORTFOLIO.recommendation: Add ATT1 at risk_mult=0.5 and ASM1 at risk_mult=1.0. Expected combined DD to drop to ~14-16%. ATT1's high frequency is the main DD contributor.
